Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: I booked a studio with a king size bed, but instead of checking in at building A, I checked in at building B, which is adjacent to the road, and I received a much better and larger apartment with two rooms and two beds. I had a spacious living room and an island-style kitchen, and there was a children's room along with a private bathroom and shower room. I couldn't have asked for a better stay for six days. However, the swimming pool was a bit short, and the Petronas Towers were somewhat blocked from view due to the nearby building, but the view of the KL Tower and Merdeka Tower was great, and it was suitable for cooling off from the heat during the day. It took about 20 minutes to walk in slippers from Suria Mall to the Petronas Towers in the late afternoon, which was comfortable enough for some exercise. I had sushi and rolls for dinner and breakfast, and I had a great time. If I go back, I would want to return to this place because it was the best value accommodation during my 15-night stay in Malaysia.

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: Honestly, at this price, this size and quality of accommodation? It doesn't make sense. The room is very spacious and feels like it could be suitable for parties, but it seems everyone thinks the same, so parties are prohibited. It’s not a hotel but a luxury residence being used like a hotel. So, check-in is done by staff at the lobby sofa on the first floor with a list to check names (not done at the reception). If you are not someone who frequently calls the reception or requests housekeeping, there are no major inconveniences. The infinity pool is also very beautiful and nice (it’s quite long, which is good for swimming, and has a great view). The accommodation is extremely good and spacious for the price, so I definitely plan to revisit. However, it's a bit disappointing that a new building next door blocks the view of the famous tower, but it's still good value for money. The room has floor-to-ceiling windows and includes a shoe cabinet (very spacious), kitchen, living room (sofa + TV + table), bedroom, bathroom, and dressing room, providing ample living space. However, since there is no desk, if you need to work during your stay on a business trip, you'll have to consider that. The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a very nice view in the morning and at night. Highly recommended!

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: Located in Platinum Suites just right beside The Face Suites (different building and reception—always ask). I was waiting for almost 30 mins to check in as it was peak holiday season but the receptionist was cooperative. In case you wonder, they will ask you deposit around RM500 in cash (returned when check out) or credit card (will be returned in weeks depending on issuer). I was assigned to 46th floor unit and surprised by the huge room with huge windows allow you to overview KL skyline. Location itself was quite strategic, walking distance to MRL Bukit Nanas just 2 stations away from Bukit Bintang and you can take 15 mins car ride to KL Sentral. What I appreciate the most is their service. I was troubled with the washing machine and told the staff to help with it. After 20 mins of waiting, they come with a new washing machine to replace the broken one and it works fine. All done with no charge. It also provides complete kitchen set, water heater, iron + board and hair dryer. I recommend you to try this property!

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: The booking process through Agoda was streamless and efficient. Quite hassle-free. The location was very nice. Right in the middle of the city and tourist spots. The unit was superb. It is very spacious and perfect for families. The cleanliness was commendable. It was fully furnished and comfortable. The only catch was the receptionist in charge of checking us in and checking us out. They did not have a real desk to entertain guests., They merely had a ledge and a sofa. Not very hospitable. They would benefit from a basic hospitality course. Speaks too fast and even got irked when I requested that my elevator key card be recalibrated since it wasn't functioning. Upon check out they said it would take 30 minutes to check my room. I was literally the only one who checked out 3 hrs before the standard time. Only when I said our van is already in the parking lot did they heed my request. After 5 minutes they said the checking was done and I was clear.30 minutes versus 5 minutes for two staff who were just sitting in the sofa, with no guests, busy with their cellphones. This was the only thing that needs improvement.

Facilities are good.Again, the put-off factor at the swimming pool is female swimmers using it are not wearing proper swimming attire even though they are fully clothed. Location is great as it is near to public transportation, shopping and eateries

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: For this hotel-apartment experience, there are some points which were lacking. Although for solo travelling, it is my diligence to always be well-prepared for such situations. Check-in process may be **tedious for those who are not mobile-savvy**. Upon entering the room, some amenities were missing (not refilled) and the room smelled very musty (room probably had been left vacant for awhile) as the room itself was so far inside the D zone of the building - where lifts at that area were inaccessible. Had to communicate w the Agent-Service team via WhatsApp to get the items that were lacking which took some time. I liked the spaciousness of the room. Bath water temp was not warm enough though. Agent-provider should have some Room Sanitizers/deodorants for better comfort of guests and better water-heating. Prime location located in the heart of the City. (Agoda - Yet to receive back my deposit of 500RM)

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: The only thing that annoyed me for this hotel stay was the check in where unlike other hotels I used to stay with my partner I had to make sure the booking name was the same as the passport and my booking wasn't and there was so much delay having to call Agoda's customer service to change it after reaching late due to traffic and my baby was tired and my helper, me and my husband were all hungry and tired. Other than that all was quite good as the shelf was broken before we stayed and the customer service for the hotel was kind enough to fix it without charging us and housekeeping was just a text away. They also fixed the shower head without charging us as it was broken before we stayed also. They also helped us with the washing machine when my helper first used it and wasn't sure how it works.

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: I'm not sure if all of these rooms are the same but mine and my friends rooms were more like an apartment and not a hotel room which is fine. They are very large. However, basic things like hand towels and hand soap do not appear to be standard here. This is clearly a much older building and you can tell by the wear and tear and cleanliness or lack thereof. They have a pool, however there does not seem to be any towels for the pool. And a very strange practice for me, which I have not experienced before and I have traveled the world is the necessity for your name and your Agoda booking to be exactly like that in your passport. Apparently for security's sake, which makes no sense given there's a photo of you and your first and last name.

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: Absolutely not worth it. The hotel only gives free parking for ONE car, and the second car needs to pay RM15 for just 3 hours. What a joke. And the parking isn’t even at the same block as the room, so imagine dragging bags and walking far while bringing an elderly person. great “hospitality”, right? For RM300 a night, room just ok. Nothing special, nothing memorable. except the disappointment. The whole place feels more like an overcrowded foreign hostel. pool full of foreigners, lifts full, lobby full. If you’re Malaysian, be prepared to feel like a tourist in your own country, except worse - because you’re treated like one. The highlight of the nonsense? I asked the receptionist a simple question in Malay about where the lift was. She replied, “English please.” Wow. In MALAYSIA. A Malaysian guest speaking the national language… and still gets told to speak English. If that’s the standard here, maybe they should just change the flag at the entrance too. Overpriced, overrated, and over-attitude. Malaysians, you deserve better. Skip this place unless you enjoy paying premium prices to be disrespected in your own country.

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: The room is very large, with good sound insulation, and there are two bathrooms, making it very convenient for our family. I really like the kitchen, which provides good utensils, but there are no chopsticks, making it a bit inconvenient to cook instant noodles. There is also a more serious issue; the non-stick pan is too old, with many scratches, making it unsafe to use, so I strongly recommend replacing it. I also hope that future guests will take care of the cookware. The rooftop pool and gym make the vacation experience more enjoyable, but the elevator lacks ventilation, and many guests crowd together, making it stuffy. Lastly, I would like to thank the beautiful lady at the front desk and the staff who cleaned our room, for ensuring we had a smooth and comfortable vacation experience.

Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250
Original_Review: I chose this hotel based on its 8.3 “Excellent” rating, but unfortunately, my experience didn’t live up to the expectations. Parking was a nightmare – Traffic and parking situation is very poorly managed. The area around the hotel is extremely congested, with cars stopping and waiting everywhere, making it very difficult to get through. Do note that you will need to check in first and get the access card for you to get free parking. Cleanliness needs improvement – the room wasn’t properly cleaned, with visible stains on the sofa and smelly towels provided. This left a bad first impression. Access issues – the hotel access card was faulty, and I had to rely on a physical key to enter the room. This was inconvenient and unacceptable for a hotel in this price range. The lift situation was frustrating – extremely long wait times to go up or down, especially during peak hours. This really tested my patience. Pool experience was unpleasant – the security staff at the pool were unnecessarily rude. You’re also required to bring your own towel if you want to access the pool area for a swim, which wasn’t made clear beforehand. If not, they won’t allow you to enter the pool area.. Overall, it was not a pleasant stay. There are serious operational and maintenance issues that need urgent attention. I hope management takes this feedback seriously.

They dont allow u to walk in swimming suits, u have to cover up yourself once u leave the pool. But The thing is.. there are no robes or extra towels in the room for guests who want to go swimming. Original_Review: Awesome place. Very spacious and big. It is a Service Apartment conjoined together with hotel. The lifts can be a big issue. Will come back again maybe this time I will stay in the suites 2 instead :))

Original_Review: I was worried because there weren't many Korean reviews, but it was unnecessary. The hotel was very nice, and I was satisfied with the friendly and excellent service from the staff. The room and bathroom were spacious and well-maintained, and the automatic blinds provided a modern feel. The minibar in the room was fully complimentary, and I used the laundry service, which responded quickly. Most guests were from Europe or China, and there were only a few Koreans, but communication in English was smooth, so there were no issues. If I had to mention one downside, it would be that the area for stopping vehicles at the main entrance of the hotel is a bit narrow. Other than that, I was very satisfied overall and would love to visit again.

Would definitely stay here again the next time we’re in KL and highly recommend this hotel for special occasions or even a relaxing city getaway.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: I had 3 night stay alone in this place. Location is good as you can just walk to Pavilion shopping mall and grab taxis are so easy to take. Breakfast selection is good but coffee quality could be improved. Service was fast and efficient . Pool bar view at night is magnificent and remind me Sevva old bar from Hong Kong Mandarin Hotel. Room size was huge and everything was great and also pillow didn’t break my neck which was plus. Found some hair in the bathroom but not a big deal . Room number 1125 had issue with drainage and engineer came fo fix immediately but still water stays and couldn’t fix 100% percent. Overall my stay was good and door men were always smiling and canvassing with customers in great manner. I will re stay in if I visit KL in the future. Thank you.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: This is my first time here and we cannot wait to stay here again in a few months time. The room was spacious and luxurious. We had our towels changed every day and every request to the concierge was executed perfectly. But most importantly, we never had hotel staffs so friendly and welcoming. Traffic right outside the hotel is not so congested unlike other hotels. Although on the weekends it might get loud as thought there might be a club somewhere. So expect some noises around 2-3am. The break first was not bad too, sometimes lacking the finesse but the mood will set you well. Lastly, most if not all amenities are child friendly. If you drive, parking is free at all times.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: Finally got to visit The RuMa Hotel to celebrate a special occasion with my partner. Check in and check out were swift and all the staffs are so accommodating, welcoming and friendly. We got upgraded to the grand king room from deluxe king and was presented a complimentary cake for our celebration. We also added on the In House Privilege program which was really top notch! Food and drinks were great and free flow except alcoholic beverages. The tacos and sliders were our personal favourite. We spent quite some time at the gym too which was not very big but sufficient to do all my workouts comfortably. Staffs were all so attentive, they will be on standby to provide any assistance needed and giving out towels and water to all the guest on floor. Would definitely come back again. Shout out to the manager at Seven Lobby Bar, my partner and I felt so at ease and had great conversations that was a highlight of our evening.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: Great location. Some views of the Petronas Towers from the pool but not brilliant. Easy to walk to them from the hotel though. Decor was amazing. Everything was presented beautifully, almost like an art gallery. The room was spacious and clean and bed was super comfortable. Facilities were good. Used the gym and pool and both did the job. The restaurant serves tasty food and bar has good selection of drinks. Staff all very pleasant if not a little too professional sometimes. The AC was pretty cold in the evening leading us to a quick retreat to bed. Would definitely stay here again though.

Original_Review: My stay at The Ruma Hotel was truly exceptional from start to finish. Upon arrival, I was pleasantly surprised with a room upgrade, which set the tone for a wonderful experience. The staff were a highlight ,warm, professional, and genuinely helpful. Their attentiveness made me feel welcomed and cared for throughout my stay. Every interaction was kind and thoughtful, and their service felt both sincere and seamless. The hotel itself is beautifully designed, elegant and calming, offering a luxurious yet comfortable atmosphere. My upgraded room was spacious, spotless, and thoughtfully equipped. Breakfast was another standout ,delicious, fresh, and diverse, with a mix of local and international selections. It was the perfect way to start each day. The Ruma isn’t just a hotel, it’s a sanctuary. I would absolutely return and highly recommend it to anyone visiting KL.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: I enjoyed my stay in this hotel because of its great location and cleanliness. I could easily walk to KLCC and Pavilion from the hotel. The hotel staff vacuumed and mopped my room daily. It was great with air purifier to keep the air clean. The room was spacious. The drinks in the fridge were complimentary. They replenished them daily. The price paid might be high but it was considered value for money looking at the walking distance to KLCC and Pavilion, high level of cleanliness and complimentary drinks in the fridge daily. It was a hassles free stay for me.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: Love everything about the RuMa for a romantic staycation with your partner! Room-big, beautiful, romantic vibe complete with he and she sinks and bathtub. Staffs-super friendly and welcoming from the moment you entering the premise, check in with the welcome drinks, during breakfast and at the pool. Received a complimentary cake for my honeymoon 🥺 Thanks guys! Location-in the heart of kl, walkable to anywhere-klcc, pavilion. 7/11 is 4mins walking towards kl convention centre. Parking-complimentary, within premise, just validate every time you want to go out at concierge. Overall, romantic , modern vibe with local Malaysian style (nasi lemak sambal sotong at breakfast is delicious)!! Will come back with my partner!!

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: I have a strong appreciation for art, and as a Malaysian, I am genuinely proud to introduce this hotel to tourists visiting Kuala Lumpur. The room is thoughtfully designed, with many lovely details that make the stay feel special—though the in-room snackbar could be more refined. The staff are polite and attentive, but the real highlight for me was the breakfast spread. It was exceptional. From the teh tarik corner to the nasi lemak, kuih-muih etc. it truly showcased our local culture and made me feel incredibly proud to be Malaysian. The pool and gym were equally impressive. I worked out during my stay and found the gym fully equipped, with thoughtful touches such as apples, chilled boxed water, and towels readily available. An amazing experience overall—amazing, amazing, amazing. I will definitely be back!

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: We were greeted by Ming. Very polite, good customer service and made us feel welcome. I havent been to KL since covid. Ming definitely made us feel super welcome. Their service from my day 1 till my check out is top notch. Room is clean, cosy and has a very nice smell. There is only 1 issue for me. The bed is too soft. But thats an individual opinion. Always looking forward to be back to the room after a long day out. Really appreciate the small gesture where staff did the folded sheets at the corner with glass & plain water at the side table and blinds all down by the time we came back to the room in the evening. Preparing guest to have a “downtime”. Love love the ambiance of the whole concept of the hotel. Definitely coming back here if i were to visit KL again! Location is great! Walking distance to KLCC and pavi. Good job on your services Ruma! I really applaud u guys for doing a great service!

Pool was nice but small, lounging area nice and cozy with good amount of seating. Wished the pool opened to at least 10pm instead of 9pm though. Family and friends have stayed before so definitely recommend it to anyone! A top choice for a very worthy rate.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: We had a really lovely time at Ruma Hotel. The staff were amazing, friendly and clearly well-trained. They really put a lot of thought in guests experience. Huge pluses were the early check-in, ( super grateful) the beautiful room with an incredible view, and the awesome breakfast spread. A few heads-up, especially for families: the walls and doors are a bit thin, so you can hear hallway noise and doors closing. The room wasn't fully soundproof, so we could hear activity from the hallway and neighboring doors. The main door lacks a soft-close mechanism, which resulted in some loud bangs. Also, the street noise at night with loud cars might wake up light sleepers (it did our baby!). The only real hiccup was our luggage taking a while because we didn't get luggage tags at check-in. ( which we weren’t informed/made aware) All in all, a fantastic hotel with superb service. Thank you for the memorable stay!
Translated_Review: We had a really lovely time at Ruma Hotel. The staff were amazing, friendly and clearly well-trained. They really put a lot of thought in guests experience. Huge pluses were the early check-in, ( super grateful) the beautiful room with an incredible view, and the awesome breakfast spread. A few heads-up, especially for families: the walls and doors are a bit thin, so you can hear hallway noise and doors closing. The room wasn't fully soundproof, so we could hear activity from the hallway and neighboring doors. The main door lacks a soft-close mechanism, which resulted in some loud bangs. Also, the street noise at night with loud cars might wake up light sleepers (it did our baby!). The only real hiccup was our luggage taking a while because we didn't get luggage tags at check-in. ( which we weren’t informed/made aware) All in all, a fantastic hotel with superb service. Thank you for the memorable stay!

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: Our stay was exceptional. Staff was attentive. Breakfast buffet although not as big as other hotels, had many varieties. They had the turndown service to prepare for the night which was a pleasant surprise after a long day. Convenient location. Walking distance to Bukit Bintang, Pavillion, Suria KLCC via the bridge near the Convention Centre. Hotel was calm and very zen. Comfy bed and pillows. The bathtub was love. Will definitely be back! I had to deduct one star from the staff component. When we arrived, the hotel had a really nice smell. We wanted to know the type of fragrance used and asked the staff who was checking us in. He was surprised there was a smell and said it might be food smell. It was definitely not food.

Original_Review: Well located, discrete and clean. The room was well appointed and immaculate. We enjoyed the Library bar and extensive gin selection, as well as the pool bar with friends. Despite the diminutive exterior the hotel is surprisingly luxurious internally. The breakfast is a solid offering, and all staff are courteous and helpful. Very close to the Fahrenheit mall and 10 minutes on foot from a metro. Unfortunately did not have time to avail ourselves of the spa or Truefitt and Hill this time, will be back!

The hotel interior design was well thought, got classical and heritage vibes. Strategic location, walking distance to Pavilion shopping mall. Oh, the hotel buffet breakfast was great. A highly recommend hotel.

Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450
Original_Review: I booked the hotel for my parents who were celebrating their 45th wedding anniversary. They are in their 60s. The staff was great and accommodated my parents early arrival and even gave them a cake. My folks gave rave reviews of the hotel, from the breakfast to the overall decor and cleanliness. The only small feedback would be to have accessible rooms available with grab bars etc for elderly folks and for more slip free mats to be made available for guests who are elderly or patrons with limited mobility. My dad had a minor slip and fall, as the tiles were slippery and there were no grab bars either. That's the only suggestion I have for the more than stellar accommodation.

Original_Review: Everything was okay overall. the hotel staff are very friendly and helpful, and my room was also very spacious and comfortable. Another plus point is that this hotel has one of the best breakfasts I’ve had in Malaysia, many other 5-star hotels had food so bad I couldn’t even eat it. As for the downsides: although the room is beautiful, the items provided in the room reduce its overall value. For example, the kettle, cups, iron, etc., are of very poor quality and practically unusable. I find this quite disappointing. In addition, the housekeeping was not thorough, which made the room dusty in several areas, such as the headboard, inside the drawers, and low areas like the space for indoor slippers.

Original_Review: I am very disappointed with my recent stay at The RuMa Hotel and Residences. Firstly, the noise level was unacceptable for a hotel of this standard. At around 10am, there were loud knocking sounds coming from the level above, which was very disruptive. More concerning, at approximately 2am, I could clearly hear doors being slammed repeatedly along the corridor, making it difficult to rest. My wife and I are not light sleepers, so you can imagine how loud and disturbing the noise must have been. Secondly, I attempted to lodge a complaint through the hotel’s WhatsApp channel during my stay, but unfortunately received no response at all. This lack of communication and support was extremely frustrating. Overall, the experience did not meet expectations in terms of comfort and service. I hope the management takes these issues seriously and improves both noise control and guest communication

Original_Review: A nice place with a short distance to Pavilion and KLCC. The pool is pleasant, though it may feel crowded with more than 10 guests. Staff are attentive and friendly. I would consider returning in the future. Unfortunately, I had to check out in the middle of the night due to my child needing emergency hospital admission, so I didn’t get the chance to try the facilities or breakfast. I made an enquiry to see if a special rate could be extended for a future stay, considering the situation. The response mentioned that management had approved offering the iPrefer rate to me. However, based on that reply, I would have appreciated a more straightforward response, there’s no need to involve management approval for a standard rate like iPrefer. A simple “no” would have been clearer and more transparent.

Original_Review: The goods: - excellent location being attached to Sunway Pyramid mall & easy short drive to Sunway university & Inti college area - relatively cheap compared to the rest in the same area - Big room - comfortable bed - kitchen with basic utensils to boil water, fridge to store food & some cooking with microwave & stove To improve: - the maintenance of the room is below mediocre: broken headboard light held together with masking tape, kitchen drawer that can hardly pull out, shower head that leaks sideway, power points that don't work, derailed wardrobe door etc - paid parking by the mall parking rate, no concessions - no wifi available - zero communication about check out procedure, no way to communicate to get help during our 3 nite stay, no one to reply the phone or WhatsApp message

Original_Review: The study abroad destination for my child is nearby, and it is directly connected to the Sunway Pyramid Hotel, making it quite convenient. There is a bathtub, but there is no stopper, so I can't fill it with water. The hot water doesn't come out. The lighting in the room is broken in several places, making it dangerous. The remotes for the TV and air conditioner are out of batteries. The air conditioner is covered in mold. The electric stove did not work. I was able to use the refrigerator and microwave. I have some complaints about the facilities, but I think the cost-performance ratio is good. I will definitely consider booking here again next time. I hope the facilities will be improved by then.

The unit looks beautiful and has a huge Samsung TV with a great Sony Bluetooth soundbar , but it's so dusty. I did not have a good sleep the whole night as i keep sneezing. The decoration feature on the wall by the bed has thick dust. The cleaner must have missed cleaning that whole wall for a very long time. Aircon was not cold enough, but the owner provided a tower fan which helps, again, the tower fan is so dusty and it is so loud when its turn to high and medium. Bathtub was not clean properly as i can see water molds by the side of the tub. This place actually can improve a lot just by having proper cleaning.

Tabletop were ripped and I accidentaly hurt my hand while trying to get my phone due to sudden blackout. After calling 3 times, the owner finally move me to another room which was slightly better in terms of electricity. No more flickering lamps and ripped table card. Only then the aircon was too noisy. I booked for 3 nights and asked if I could get any refund but got no responds. Since it's non refundable booking, I decided to book another hotel. Definitely not worth it.

Original_Review: My first time here at Sunway Pyramid Hotel and it has impressed me so much! The standards are impeccable indeed; very clean rooms and bathrooms, very safe and secure, and the best part is the fast accessibility to the mall! Makes this trip so worth it! Despite a minor hiccup with booking details between Agoda and the Hotel's records, the hotel resolved it quickly and managed to cater to our specific requests. Breakfast was delightful and offered a huge spread of local and international foods that satisfies any palate, with vegan and vegetarian options available. The Hotel sits right next to the themepark and it is quite an interesting place to spend time in with family, young and old. It even offers an actual beach in the middle of it, which has simulated waves for surfing! Amazing really. There is a wildlife zoo that features lions and tigers even, as well as a multitude of fun rides, including go-karting! A definite recommendation for any traveller coming to Subang Jaya for the first time.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: Was checking into the Sunway Pyramid Hotel and was very pleasantly surprised to find out that we were given an upgrade to the Sunway Resort! The staff were very helpful in bringing our luggage from one hotel to the other, check-in was smooth, and they offered complimentary cookies to our daughter. Our room was originally a deluxe room with a park view, so imagine our surprise that we still had the park view we wanted. The room was spacious and comfortable. The hotel was also connected to the mall so very convenient too. The breakfast was impressive and held a wide selection of cuisine, from local delights to Arabic food. The live cook station staff were also very friendly and overall food was delicious. The kids buffet section was a big hit, and very thoughtful of the hotel to have toddler sized tables and chairs. The hotel pool was spacious and also had a view of the Sunway Lagoon park. My daughter enjoyed the Waterventure pool on the 5th floor as well as the play area there. Overall, would definitely recommend staying there!

The location is very strategic and the service at the counter is very friendly. They are always attentive to the needs of customers and ready to provide service on time. I can see a beautiful view outside the window of the room. The colorful lights along the road are attractive. The only downside is that the location for the breakfast buffet requires a walk to the next building, Sunway Resort, to check in there. Luckily, there were staff who took the sign at the Sunway Resort lobby in the early morning to prevent getting lost or taking the wrong lift. This deserves praise. If you want to stay here, there's really no need to bring a car; it's easy to find food walking around. The cleanliness here is definitely top-notch, with staff always on duty anywhere.

Overall, this hotel is very suitable for a short getaway to rest, business trips, or even emergency stays. Its comfort, convenience, and strategic location make it a reliable choice whenever you need a place to stay in the area.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: Sunway Pyramid Hotel has an amazing location, especially for solo travelers like me, it’s right inside a big shopping mall and it’s also perfect for families and kids. It honestly feels like a mini theme park city. There’s a huge mall, water park, ice skating and so many food options, you’ll never feel bored or alone. I was sad that I only stayed for 2 nights because I didn’t realize how great the area was until I got there. Yes, the hotel is a bit old m, but I can’t complain at all. The room was spacious, very clean and had a desk which was perfect for using my laptop and getting some work done. Most of all, I felt safe and welcomed. The staff are friendly and kind. The atmosphere gives you a warm feeling, like you belong there. And the best part? You never feel lonely in this hotel.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: First off, in terms of value for money, it’s definitely more affordable compared to many of the high-end hotels in KL & PJ. You get a very decent standard of comfort and convenience without paying premium city hotel prices! Location-wise, it’s hard to beat. The hotel is literally a stone’s throw away from Sunway Lagoon, it’s also directly connected to Sunway Pyramid, so food & shopping are all just a short walk away — super convenient. The staff were very friendly and professional. Check-in was smooth and impressively fast, which is always a great start to a stay. I also managed to secure a late checkout. Everything was near to perfect, from comfort to convenience. The only downside was spotting a baby cockroach running around in the toilet :(

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: I booked this room for my honeymoon stay. Called the staff in advance to inform. But during check in, the receptionist did not get the remark. At least i got a card and some chocolates. I booked a room with a city view but received a room with a view facing the inside of the mall. No access to balcony either. Parking flat rate RM15 is nice but would be better if complimentary. Toilet is very small. Breakfast cafe is so far away. Nothing much playing on TV. Staff members are nice and helpful. Room is big. Bed is huge and comfy. Location is great; wakking distance to everything. Maybe check out Sunway Resort instead.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: It’s linked to the giant Sunway mall that has a supermarket, indoor playground, ice skating rink, arcade, and countless top restaurants, as well as Sunway lagoon theme park. Although Sunway pyramid hotel doesn’t have its own swimming pool and gym, it’s just a 5min walk through a shopping mall to Sunway Resort hotel’s which you can now use for free if you are a GHA member (free sign up as of writing). As a member you can also use Sunway Resort’s water playground for a nominal fee per child (as of writing). Having arrived late with young children we were very grateful for ***’s promptness and kindness at the check in counter as well as all the others at the concierge. The room we got was spacious, clean, and well maintained. Only one little snag was that it took about an hour for us to get a baby cot after we had checked in, but for the next two nights the room service staff attended to our needs within 5-10 minutes. Overall it was a super stay and we hope it’s price doesn’t go up from more people finding out about this gem

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: I enjoyed staying here, there are many facilities and the staff are friendly and kind. I booked 2 rooms with my family and requested a room with a connecting door, which was provided without any additional charge. Breakfast was indeed moved to Sunway Resort, but the location is not far as it was directed by the staff, and we just needed to go down the escalator, so it was not tiring. There was an abundance of food for breakfast to the point that we were confused about what to eat 😃. At checkout, we left our luggage because we wanted to go to Sunway Lagoon until the afternoon, and the staff helped us find transportation. Overall, all the staff were very helpful. The hotel is close to the mall, no need to go outside, in front of the hotel there are many food options and supermarkets. My child always says that the food at Sunway Hotel is better than elsewhere haha. If I travel to Malaysia again, Sunway Pyramid Hotel will be my choice for accommodation.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: Sunway is an idea location for families! It's attached to the mall for shopping and dining, and has access to the Lagoon theme park right behind it. Location is quite a bit outside the city center, but we didn't mind since we only came to KL for a conference over the weekend. Staff was very professional at all times and service was spectacular. The bed was firm, yet comfortable, and the room was the perfect size. The breakfast/dining area was shared with the sister-hotel, Sunway Resort, and was quite tricky to find. But WOW -- it had the best selection of food choices I've seen in a breakfast buffet! We'd be very happy to stay at this hotel again.

Original_Review: I had a seminar at Sunway Conference Centre so Sunway Pyramid Hotel was the best option to stay in. At check in time I request a middle room & high floor, both requests were fulfilled by a male concierge name Tuesday. I’m so grateful for his arrangement. Room was clean, bed is big. Toilet & bathroom is clean, just a little outdated with old stains. They provided 2bottles of glass filtered water for a single night stay which I truly appreciate. Buffer was at the next door Sunway Resort with great selections. Definitely worth the money.

Original_Review: I recently stayed at Harbour Grand Kowloon and was impressed by the elegant ambience, spacious rooms, and stunning harbor views. The service was courteous and efficient throughout the stay, and the hotel’s facilities, including the rooftop pool and fitness center, were top-notch. However, the food was somewhat underwhelming. While the presentation was decent, the overall taste and variety did not quite meet the expectations set by the hotel’s otherwise high standards. There’s definitely room for improvement in the culinary department. besides, hotel amenities need to purchase separately as the hotel provide less of essential amenities like tooth brush , drinking water, etc. Still, if you’re looking for a comfortable and scenic stay in Hong Kong with great service and amenities, Harbour Grand Kowloon is a solid choice — just don’t expect the food to be a highlight.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: We checked in on 23/2 and initially had a pleasant experience. The facilities and overall environment were good, and our family, including our baby, were enjoying the stay. However, after returning to our room at night from shopping, we noticed a strong cigarette smell in the room. We immediately contacted the front desk to report the issue. We were informed that it might be due to the air circulation system and that nothing could be done at that time. Unfortunately, the cigarette smell persisted throughout the night and was still present the next morning around 7am+. This was particularly concerning for us as we had specifically booked a non-smoking room and are traveling with a baby. We believe that guests who reserve non-smoking rooms should be assured of a smoke-free environment. We kindly request that the hotel investigate this matter seriously and take the necessary steps to prevent similar situations in the future. Aside from this issue, the rest of our stay has been satisfactory. We hope the hotel will look into this matter and provide an appropriate response. Thank you for your attention.

While the hotel doesn’t have its own swimming pool, GHA Discovery members get complimentary access to the pool at Sunway Resort Hotel, which was a really nice bonus. There’s also free pool access at Sunway Lagoon Hotel, but be prepared for a 15-minute walk. One tip: be mindful of where you park. Preferred parking in Sunway is convenient but can cost twice the rate of regular parking per day. Overall, a comfortable and enjoyable stay, especially for those who want everything within walking distance.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: Got a surprise upgrade to Sunway Resort and all hand over process was done so smoothly. The suite was so spacious the kids were just excited about it. Easy access to Sunway Lagoon. Ultra busy traffic during breakfast at the coffee house but staff was very calm despite being given the 'black face' by customers. The only downside is I couldn't get a baby cot although I requested during booking, so yeah, I didn't sleep well. Bed was really comfy with enough pillows. Ooh and if you plan to stay late in Sunway Lagoon, which we did till 8ish pm, the direct exit to hotel is not 'available'. Would recommend to check on that before booking. We walked all the way through the mall, through another hotel n back to Sunway Resort after our water activities. Embarrassed.. hahaha. But still a very good stay for us.. thank you

Original_Review: Enjoyed our stay at Sunway Pyramid. Appreciated the adjoining rooms accorded to us, as can easily assist with the caring of my very young grandkids next door. Easily accessible to a massive shopping mall that housed a supermarket, numerous restaurants/fast food outlets, game zones, and even an ice skating ring. I didn't even need to utilise any transport from the hotel, apart from going to the airport, because most facilities/shoping items can be found within walking distance from our hotel. Breakfast at the hotel displayed a huge variety of dishes. Plus it was so easy to walk over to Sunway Lagoon for the activities.

Guests are advised to use the complimentary swimming pool facilities at Sunway Hotel, or alternatively, access the pool at Sunway Resort for a fee which, in our view, was relatively costly.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: I recently stayed at the hotel for Pinkfish and had a mostly positive experience. The room was really, really nice – clean, spacious, and well-appointed with all the amenities we needed for a comfortable stay. The hotel's location is excellent, with a shopping mall downstairs making things very convenient for dining and shopping. However, we did encounter a significant inconvenience with the parking system. Despite paying the flat rate hotel guest parking fee at the lobby, when we tried to exit the parking lot, the system indicated that I hadn't made the payment. I called the hotel and was put on hold, which was frustrating. Ultimately, I had to walk all the way back to the lobby to resolve the issue. This was really, really inconvenient and put a damper on an otherwise pleasant stay. Overall, our stay was good, but I hope the hotel can improve their parking system to prevent similar issues for future guests because this is the second time this has happened to us.

Room curtain & light can adjust type of radiant/cozy/calm/relax via touch screen smart home system beside our bed. Bed are king size & very comfortable, the pillow have few type which is Natural Buckwheat/Bamboo Charcoal/Memory Foam etc.. Coffee machine, espresso coffee, tea bags, 2 glass of water, 2 chocolate. Bath tub with jacuzzi and tv screen. I noticed that hotel provide Harman Kardon bluetooth speaker on the desk. Overall, it was great for me..

Original_Review: First time and never again. Checked in to a bigger room with balcony and kitchenette The furnitures and bathroom vanity are quite old, leaking from ceiling near spotlight caused our bedtime to be disrupted with power supply being cut off. Facilities staff came to resolved and we informed him about the leaking, which may have contributed to the power trip. We were relocated to another room and seems a little better but no key card due to some technical issue. So we stayed in the room the whole of next day for to avoid the hassle of having the concierge escort us back and forth using their master keycard. On our second last day, our key card could not access for some reason so we had to again go back to the concierge to resolved the keycard issue. Huge disappointment for a stay at sunway pyramid. We enjoyed the stay at sunway resort 2 years ago so we decided to try Sunway Pyramid but this time was a totally opposite experience. The only thing we enjoyed was the sunway lagoon and shopping mall trips.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500
Original_Review: Stayed at Sunway Pyramid Hotel mainly for easy access to Sunway Lagoon, and for that, it’s a solid choice. The good: The hotel is directly connected to Sunway Pyramid Mall, which is excellent for food and shopping – endless options. You can watch ice hockey for free in the evening if you’re interested – a fun bonus. Super close to Sunway Lagoon. In fact, you could even come back to your room in your wet clothes if you forget a change (okay, kidding… but technically true 😄). The not-so-good: Rooms are a bit small. Not many facilities inside the room Parking costs around 16 ringgit per day, which adds up Verdict: If you’re planning to spend most of your time at Sunway Lagoon or the mall, this is a very convenient base. Just don’t expect luxury space or free parking.

Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: I had few occasions staying at Centerstage hosted by Perfect Host. The room is exceptionally good, to compare with the low price. Name what you get at 4* hotel...you should be getting them here. Utensils, toiletries, good and friendly staff, clean and comfy bed, and best part is you can enjoy watching Netflix here. You can easily get your laundry done at the ground floor and mynews is also located down there to help you on your retail purchase. However, I would like to recommend the host to check on their wifi /Internet speed. It seems like the speed was slightly snailing during ny last stay there....Or maybe the speed is according to the floor level? Because sometimes I don't experienced this. Anyway to conclude. Thumbs up to Perfect Host.

Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: Pros - A/C system are working perfectly - Body wash, shampoo and hand wash were provided - Basic dining sets are provided (without dishwashing soap) - Room is clean and tidy - Additional pillows were provided - WiFi system are working perfectly - 2 bottles of complimentary 500 mL drinking water were provided, and a selection of coffee and tea with sugar sachet as well - Customer service are excellent - A selection of grocery stores, restaurants and ATM are available at the ground floor area Cons - small shower room size - Stagnant water in the shower room during shower - Bed is not a real king size bed, it’s just a two single bed that was assembled together, thus there’s a tendency for the beds to be separated during resting - Kettle lid could not be closed - Exhaust fan in the shower room isn’t functioning - A smart TV is available inside the unit, but the TV response to the command that was transmitted from the remote control are very slow, and Netflix subscription is not available - Mini fridge is not so cool anymore - The room is not fully soundproofed, mainly on the window and entry door - Daily parking pass is not available, in comparison to their business counterpart (Greystone) that’s able to provide the aforementioned pass for a mere RM10 to their guest. Thus, Perfect Host guest has to either pay a hefty parking fee to secure an on site parking spot or to park their car at a public parking lot around the premise that comes with security concern - The tall cabinet that was situated beside the vanity table could not be closed/opened easily due to lack of space and poor installation

Translated_Review: The price of the room here is cheap, for one night you can get under rm100. The room is clean, the aircond is cold, the iron kettle works, but I can't watch the TV. There are only 3 things that I am not satisfied with. 1. Parking - Parking fees are very expensive. RM10 for the first 8 hours. Even 30 minutes is still rm10. I parked one night from 730pm to 8am total Rm23. When my mom parked after dropping me off less than 1 hour, she charged rm25. When calls are busy rejecting, WhatsApp doesn't even reply. 2. Gloomy Environment - I recommend installing area lights towards the elevator, and outside areas. Because there are many foreigners here, there are good ones like those who keep the counter. But the worry is hanging around outside. I waited for my mother to park the car, I sat next to KK Mart. There is a Bangladeshi man in blue street and my fortune teller. He turned back to the end of the road but was still engrossed in spying. I entered KK even from afar and he followed. But alhamdulillah it was safe to enter the elevator and to the room. 3. Cleanliness around - The elevator is a bit smelly and there is tissue waste. I hope this issue can be maintained and resolved in the near future.

Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: If you want a short stay with minimum time spend at the hotel then i think its a fine place. But for holiday not a suitable place especially with small kids. They dont clean daily but once a week. So they dont provide fresh towel daily. The staff was friendly. When we ask for them they quickly attend to us. Its just that one of my rooms the toilet was leaking so it retainsed water on the floor. The tvs in both room didnt function. They provide iron in each room, kettle, plates, fork and spoon also basic amenities. Overall ok. But just for transit or short stay. The parking is freaking expensive and it is under different management so you cant waive the fee from the hotel stay.

Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: Pros: The kitchenette was well-equipped, making it easy to cook or reheat food. All toiletries were provided, which was convenient and thoughtful. Cons: The room door was very heavy, requiring a strong slam to shut it properly—quite disturbing, especially at night. The soundproofing was poor, and I could clearly hear noises from the next room. There were also kids playing in the hallway, ringing doorbells, which got really annoying. Cleanliness was also an issue — I found hair inside the bed linen, and the pillow had a noticeable smell, which was off-putting. Lastly, the deposit refund took a very long time, which added unnecessary frustration after check-out. Overall, while there were some convenient features, the cleanliness and noise issues, along with the slow refund process, made the stay less than ideal.

There are no towels or floor mats, just go out of the bathroom and the floor is wet.. There are mosquitoes in the room. I have killed 2, there are still more, I can't chase them.. Original_Review: Parking is gonna cost a bomb, be ready for a RM20-30 bill per night OG overnight parking. Food options will be scarce, you will do better with surrounding malls. Facilities are tired and need proper plumbing and waterproofing. I stayed at the Club Floor and was floored by the water stains and seepage from the bathroom walls. Lucky it wasn’t leaking. My stay was disrupted by a very angry looking staff asking me about my check in after I had successfully checked Jin at the counter. Counter staff also was unsure of the deposit at check out. Surely team communications could be better? The only saving grace? Its location. Other matters such as value for money is negated by the parking situation and cleanliness is negated by the tiny cockroaches I detected jn their rooms.

Original_Review: Honestly I had a number of issues including check-in, login to internet and staff attitude that was really sub-par. But I was on a business trip with 3-4 meetings in nearby locations in PJ, so the location made sense. No free parking, so prepare RM20-30 daily for overnight parking. Entry ti parking is also confusing. You need to use your Credit/Dehit card only, no TNG accepted. There is one friendly and reasonably priced cafe at the Ground floor. The saving Grace of the place if you ask me. Other than this, be prepared for a business-bare minimum treatment. You’ve been warned.

Hotel_Address: Jalan 13/1, Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: Parking mahalll....(Rm45) Dari petang 3pm sampai 9:30am. Takde kopi 3in1 juga Dalam bilik. Perabot Ada yg rosak dah. But it's ok. Cuma masalah parking agak Mahal . Kedai bawah Banyak tutup. Keluar pagi, nak cepat, tengok kat kaunter pulak takde staff. Dah 9:30am...kalau nak keluar jangan lah keluar semis. Tinggal lah sorang staff. Akhirnya tinggalkan jer kunci atas meja kaunter. Tak boleh tunggu sebab nak cepat. Ermm....memang tak laa datang sini lagi. Apapun tq sudah menginap 1malam disini. Sebab nampak cantik gambar kat Agoda, tapi hampehhh
Translated_Review: Expensive parking....(Rm45) From 3pm to 9:30am. No 3in1 coffee in the room either. Some furniture is damaged. But it's ok. The only problem is parking is quite expensive. Hotel_Address: Jalan 13/1, Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: ​I have mixed feelings about my stay. On the positive side, the bed was very comfortable and I slept well. However, the experience was ruined by several maintenance and service issues.​The Bad:​Power failures: The room blacked out twice, specifically every time I used the shower.​Service: During the first blackout, the team was fast. However, during the second blackout, I was stuck in the shower with soap in my eyes. The staff member who came didn't apologize and instead just told me it was time to checkout. It was extremely unprofessional and insensitive given the situation.​Cleanliness: Parts of the room were dusty, and the corridor had a distinct smell of cockroaches.​Amenities: No coffee was provided. There was a water heater, but strangely, no glass cups to use with it.​Parking: Free parking is available outside. If you park inside, you usually pay via Touch 'n Go, but the system was down during my stay, so I was forced to use the app.​I cannot recommend this place until they fix their electrical issues and train their staff on basic hospitality

Hotel_Address: Jalan 13/1, Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200
Original_Review: Parking is expensive, my partner ended up parking outside the place, still within a walkable distance. This place requires a RM100 deposit, the staff will disappear around 12am, if you want to check out, need to leave your card on the counter, only after the staff come back in the morning they will process your deposit again a few days later. i also saw a dead cockroach in my room, kettle provided had the wrong plug, could not be used. luckily there is a mynews directly below the hotel so can get whatever you need from there. overall the location sucks, and parking isnt provided, there is also no 24 hours staffing. Would not recommend unless you are desperate and there are no other options. still a budget hotel.

Hotel_Address: 130, jalan radin anum, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 57000
Original_Review: This hotel was conveniently located near the event we needed to attend, and initially, we could only book a twin single room. Upon our arrival, we requested an upgrade, explaining that my hubby hadn’t slept the night before due to event preps and that we would prefer a queen/king size bed if avail. We were open to paying extra for the upgrade, and the staff promptly facilitated this change, providing us with a very quiet room. The front desk staff was friendly, always smiling, and even checked in on us (on our way to dinner) to ensure everything was good. The room itself was cozy, equipped with all essential amenities, and super clean + comfy. The area offers some of the best nasi lemak and Ayam Gepuk I’ve had in a long time. The cafe next to the Ayam Gepuk place also serves excellent pastries. However..be sure to plan for parking, as the hotel doesn’t cover parking fees which I overlooked when making the booking.

Hotel_Address: 130, jalan radin anum, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 57000
Original_Review: The staff, room, a/c, bathroom were all new, clean and represented good value for money. Bed was comfy. No complaints at all. The location is interesting, its certainly not bad, there were heaps of shops and restaurants right near it, but it's about 9 to 10 km away from Kuala lumper city centre. So, if you want to see KL towers and the Petronis towers you'll have to use grab or catch public transport. Batu caves were probably about 25 km away. It's actually quite far from Kuala lumper airport which is about 50 minutes drive. So, it depends on what you want to do. Stay further in the heart of Kuala Lumper where you're nearer tourist attractions but will probably add another 20-25 mins drive time to airport or stay a little further out which makes the drive to the airport quicker and the hotel a bit cheaper. Still, I'm satisfied with my choice.

Hotel_Address: 130, jalan radin anum, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 57000
Original_Review: The room was basic, nice but a bit old. The staff assisted with check-in smoothly. He was responsive and quickly changed our room upon request after we spotted a cockroach. However, there were some inconveniences- no kettle/cup though mineral water, coffee/ creamer/ sugar were provided in the room; the shower took a while to get warm; the drainage was slow and causing minor pooling. Also, the noise from the corridor could be heard clearly. Overall, the service was good, but improvements in cleanliness and maintenance would enhance the stay.

Hotel_Address: 130, jalan radin anum, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 57000
Original_Review: I want to start by giving credit where it’s due, the front desk staff are polite, patient, and available 24/7. Their attitude was good, and they genuinely tried their best to help whenever we approached them. However, the overall stay was disappointing due to the facilities and room conditions. First, the hotel does not have a lift, only a long staircase. This is extremely inconvenient for travellers with large or heavy luggage, especially after a long journey. This should be clearly highlighted beforehand. Facilities and room quality were below expectations. Room amenities are inconsistent, some rooms may have a fridge, some may not. My friend upgraded from a queen bedroom to a triple bedroom for an additional RM30 per night. While that room came with a mini fridge, it had a strong, unpleasant milk smell, likely left by previous guests. The bed was neatly arranged, but there were visible signs of dirtiness that should not be ignored. As for our queen-size bedroom, the space was extremely limited, with barely any room to move. The air conditioning was weak and did not cool the room properly. Although toiletries were provided in the toilet, there was no hair dryer in the room. Due to these issues, we checked out on the same night after returning from our travel, as we were not comfortable staying any longer. While the pricing is fair and cheap, it truly reflects the quality. You get what you pay for. The most disappointing part was the refund issue. Initially, the receptionist told us that as long as we requested a refund through Agoda, the hotel would accept it. However, after we checked out early and switched hotels, Agoda informed us that the property management refused the refund. We immediately called the hotel after receiving this message, but were told that it was “not their problem” since we chose to check out early, and they would not refund us.

Original_Review: Be willing to pay a little bit more and likely get whole better than this. The slightly cheaper price of this place is not worth the discomfort, lack of cleanliness. Location might seem better because of proximity to main road but actually it's a bottleneck. If you're waiting for a Grab you'll be waiting a long time most the time because of the jam on main road as well as on the side road leading to hotel. And if you've got a room facing this road, you won't get any rest (get earplugs). Room clearly not properly cleaned. Lots of hair and dust. Bathroom sink was scummy and rusty and lots of little cockroaches hiding under the sink. Quite off-putting. Staff are seemingly fine. Thank God for that. It's not exactly terrible. But you're better off paying a little bit more and try the next hotel on the list.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: Newly opened hotel with modern facilities and a convenient location (South). Paid around 3** ++ for a night. The room was simple yet big in size, equipped with a bed, sofa bed, working desk, chair, TV, and the basic amenities required. Cleanliness and hygiene standards were well maintained throughout the property. They have a shaded swimming pool, a breezy terrace area with a lot of tables and chairs perfect for relaxing. Car parking was charged at RM8 per day. For those who like to walk around, they have direct access to a shopping mall located at the ground floor just next door. Plenty of shops and dining options within walking distance too. Breakfast was enjoyable with a good variety of options, 7/10. What stood out most was the service provided by the staff. Mr. Nathan & the restaurant team delivered welcoming customer service and were attentive to diners' needs. Not to forget, the Marketing Manager Ms. Wee Wee (name might be spelled wrong, she’s a Chinese woman) and Assistant Exec Housekeeper Mr. Chong, who were present at the restaurant during breakfast hour, went the extra mile in providing a pleasant service recovery and showed genuine care. Mr. Devin & Ms. Diane from Front Office just did their job efficiently, ensuring that the check-in and the guest experience were handled smoothly. They were approachable and willing to assist with guests' inquiries and requests. The luggage delivery to the room experienced a slight delay. However, the matter was handled after a phone call. The Security guards stationed at the entrance showed great hospitality. They always greeted guests with smiles, promptly opened doors & maintained a welcoming presence, contributing positively to guest experience from the moment of arrival. Overall, it was a relaxing one-day stay with good facilities, convenient surroundings, and a team that showed efforts in delivering quality service. Keep improving

Thank you to the entire team for your excellent hospitality!

Original_Review: This hotel is worth your money!! I recently stayed at this hotel with my parents and we had a truly wonderful experience. From the moment we arrived, the staffs were all warm, welcoming, and extremely attentive. The check-in process was smooth. It was my parents anniversary day and the staff managed to give a surprise cake to my parents. I am glad they did! The room was spotless and very comfortable. Bathrooms are clean and no weird smells sound or clogged water. Breakfast was a highlight—fresh, delicious, and with a wide variety of options to suit all tastes. To our surprise, the paneer tikka masala was the best. Location- super convenient. The hotel is perfectly situated at old klang road. You can easily travel to mid valley or OUG. Also, the hotel is connected to a new mall Bloomsvale Shopping Galery. Lots of eateries and a 24hr grocery stall. I highly recommend this hotel and would gladly stay here again. A perfect blend of comfort, service, and convenience.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: Overall, I had a good and pleasant stay at the Hotel. The staff - from Security Personnel, Bell Boys, Receptionist to Restaurant Executives very friendly and great customers with professionalism. Room: It was spacious and for the price, its worth the penny. The room was clean, the variety of channel are good amenities in the room are complete. The bed is comfortable. I stayed at Level 20 and it was quiet (which i love the most). If you are like myself who sometime too comfortable in my own skin and prefer to have a full view of the outside, you might want to request room that are not facing to the opposite apartment/condo. Location: It was next to the Bloomsvale Shopping Gallery. I believe (with less traffic), it would take you around 40-1hrs to KL (I might be wrong but it was just 30 minutes from Axiata Arena Bukit Jalil so I guess it is not far from KL either). Food variety: Menu for in room dining is limited I would say but for breakfast it was good - both variety and taste wise. I cant remember the exact name but I found the parata/roti canai with the dal and vegetarian curry was good! The fruits are fresh too.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: Overall the stay was okay. Room was spacious and clean, bed was comfy (my son likes it very much). We asked for late checkout and was accomodated by the front desk officer, really appreciated as our flight was at 5pm. One of the bathroom light was broken and respond from Engineering team was fast, change with new lightbulb and working perfectly. Amenities provided in the bathroom is complete, shower gel and shampoo smells very nice. Breakfast was also good with may selections, and I think all the food is halal. At check out time, the concierge team also very helpful, offering to order Grab for us to the airport. Another advantage of staying here is the shopping mall located in the same building and there is a direct access elevator from 3rd floor of hotel to the shopping mall. There is a 24 hours supermarket at the shopping mall and many selections for food & drinks, it even have an indoor children playground. The only minus is location of hotel is not in the city center, we will need to drive approximately 30 minutes to city center if your plan is to visit the Twin Tower or KL Tower.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: Newly opened hotel with modern facilities and a convenient location (South). Paid around 3** ++ for a night. The room was simple yet big in size, equipped with a bed, sofa bed, working desk, chair, TV, and the basic amenities required. Cleanliness and hygiene standards were well maintained throughout the property. They have a shaded swimming pool, a breezy terrace area with a lot of tables and chairs perfect for relaxing. Car parking was charged at RM8 per day. For those who like to walk around, they have direct access to a shopping mall located at the ground floor just next door. Plenty of shops and dining options within walking distance too. Breakfast was enjoyable with a good variety of options, 7/10. What stood out most was the service provided by the staff. Mr. Nathan & the restaurant team delivered welcoming customer service and were attentive to diners' needs. Not to forget, the Marketing Manager Ms. Wee Wee (name might be spelled wrong, she’s a Chinese woman) and Assistant Exec Housekeeper Mr. Chong, who were present at the restaurant during breakfast hour, went the extra mile in providing a pleasant service recovery and showed genuine care. Mr. Devin & Ms. Diane from Front Office just did their job efficiently, ensuring that the check-in and the guest experience were handled smoothly. They were approachable and willing to assist with guests' inquiries and requests. The luggage delivery to the room experienced a slight delay. However, the matter was handled after a phone call. The Security guards stationed at the entrance showed great hospitality. They always greeted guests with smiles, promptly opened doors & maintained a welcoming presence, contributing positively to guest experience from the moment of arrival. Overall, it was a relaxing one-day stay with good facilities, convenient surroundings, and a team that showed efforts in delivering quality service. Keep improving.

Original_Review: Travel to KL for a short get away with family. Location was strategy, super clean, super spacious. Easily stay family of 4. Best deal… breakfast kids below 12yr eat for free. Occasionally we travel back hotel late sometimes or forget to get house keeping when we left the room, we just txt “Nabil” supervisor at front desk and he happily help us to arrange. Super friendly person and a big shout out to him and his team. Definitely be back and the go to hotel from now on when ever I visit. Great job Courtyard.

Original_Review: This hotel is worth your money!! I recently stayed at this hotel with my parents and we had a truly wonderful experience. From the moment we arrived, the staffs were all warm, welcoming, and extremely attentive. The check-in process was smooth. It was my parents anniversary day and the staff managed to give a surprise cake to my parents. I am glad they did! The room was spotless and very comfortable. Bathrooms are clean and no weird smells sound or clogged water. Breakfast was a highlight—fresh, delicious, and with a wide variety of options to suit all tastes. To our surprise, the paneer tikka masala was the best. Location- super convenient. The hotel is perfectly situated at old klang road. You can easily travel to mid valley or OUG. Also, the hotel is connected to a new mall Bloomsvale Shopping Galery. Lots of eateries and a 24hr grocery stall. I highly recommend this hotel and would gladly stay here again. A perfect blend of comfort, service, and convenience.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: Overall, I had a good and pleasant stay at the Hotel. The staff - from Security Personnel, Bell Boys, Receptionist to Restaurant Executives very friendly and great customers with professionalism. Room: It was spacious and for the price, its worth the penny. The room was clean, the variety of channel are good amenities in the room are complete. The bed is comfortable. I stayed at Level 20 and it was quiet (which i love the most). If you are like myself who sometime too comfortable in my own skin and prefer to have a full view of the outside, you might want to request room that are not facing to the opposite apartment/condo. Location: It was next to the Bloomsvale Shopping Gallery. I believe (with less traffic), it would take you around 40-1hrs to KL (I might be wrong but it was just 30 minutes from Axiata Arena Bukit Jalil so I guess it is not far from KL either). Food variety: Menu for in room dining is limited I would say but for breakfast it was good - both variety and taste wise. I cant remember the exact name but I found the parata/roti canai with the dal and vegetarian curry was good! The fruits are fresh too.
Translated_Review: Overall, I had a good and pleasant stay at the Hotel. Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: Overall the stay was okay. Room was spacious and clean, bed was comfy (my son likes it very much). We asked for late checkout and was accomodated by the front desk officer, really appreciated as our flight was at 5pm. One of the bathroom light was broken and respond from Engineering team was fast, change with new lightbulb and working perfectly. Amenities provided in the bathroom is complete, shower gel and shampoo smells very nice. Breakfast was also good with may selections, and I think all the food is halal. At check out time, the concierge team also very helpful, offering to order Grab for us to the airport. Another advantage of staying here is the shopping mall located in the same building and there is a direct access elevator from 3rd floor of hotel to the shopping mall. There is a 24 hours supermarket at the shopping mall and many selections for food & drinks, it even have an indoor children playground. The only minus is location of hotel is not in the city center, we will need to drive approximately 30 minutes to city center if your plan is to visit the Twin Tower or KL Tower.

The mall attached to the hotel was nice.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: We stayed at this hotel for seven nights. The one thing we genuinely liked was the bed. It was very comfortable and helped us sleep better, which is not something we usually experience. Unfortunately, the overall service and facilities were disappointing. Room service was very slow. We requested forks and spoons and had to wait a long time. We also asked for a refill of toilet paper in the bathroom, but the reception said they usually do not provide refills, which we found unreasonable for a hotel stay. We had to change rooms once due to a sewage pipe issue that caused a strong smell in the room. The reception promised to send staff to assist us with moving rooms, but we waited a long time before any help arrived. It seemed the hotel might be short-staffed. Housekeeping also came at different times each day, which made it difficult to plan our schedule. The swimming pool is poorly designed, with sharp edges and very slippery tiles that lack proper anti-slip texture. The pool is not heated, and the water was too cold to swim comfortably. The swimming facilities also feel incomplete, as there is no outdoor shower area. In addition, the steam room and sauna in the female restroom were not working during our stay. Overall, while the bed was comfortable, the service, facilities, and management need significant improvement.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: A new hotel, so the facilities are very clean and well maintained. Good location with a reasonable mall attached. Rooms are a good size with suitable amenities and nicely designed. Great view of KL too. However, check in is less than ideal, esoecially if you have young children with you. I was fortunate to arrive early (around 2pm) so I checked in quickly and was allowed to go to the pool area with my son to wait until my room was ready. When the time came to go to my room (3.30pm) I was told by lobby staff that I needed to queue up again (even though I had checked in already). The wait was at least 45 minutes going by the 25 or so people already in the queue and waiting in the lobby. I refused and eventually got my keycard to go to the room. Car parking is also confusing and when I requested to validate my car registration number, at 4.45pm, to avail of the hotel rate, I was told again I had to queue up (similar sized queue as at 3.30pm). Check out was the easiest experience, with no queue (I checked out earlier than 12noon to avoid potential queues). Staff were stony faced, not at all friendly and did not even ask if I had enjoyed my stay. This is disappointing for a hotel that is part of the Marriott group and not at all how I imagined my experience to be. Overall, a very nice hotel, with mediocre customer service.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: I booked a room for my grandparents as we had an event in the Old Klang Road area. While the room was generally clean, the mirror had noticeable fingerprints, which required me to call housekeeping for cleaning. The bed was comfortable, but the bathroom could benefit from a hand bidet, which would enhance overall comfort. Additionally, the hotel should consider offering more assistance for older guests, such as wheelchairs or other mobility aids. One major disappointment was the lack of suitable entertainment options for my grandparents. The television didn’t offer any Tamil or Indian channels, and since it wasn’t a smart TV, we were unable to connect to YouTube. The hotel expected us to bring our own HDMI cable, which I found to be unreasonable for a five-star establishment. I expected better amenities and more thoughtful entertainment options.

Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200
Original_Review: I booked this hotel as I was attending a conference in the same hotel. It looks and feels modern and clean. The breakfast offered a reasonably wide choice.
